strategic_overview: The problem is best viewed as a stable GL-character question: the Schur coefficient c_lambda(n) is the multiplicity of the GL_m irreducible of highest weight lambda in the multigraded Hilbert character of diagonal coinvariants. This is the framework of Bergeron's Multivariate diagonal coinvariant spaces for complex reflection groups, https://arxiv.org/abs/1105.4358, Theorem 2.1, and the GL_k x S_n bookkeeping in (GL_k × S_n)-Modules of Multivariate Diagonal Harmonics, https://arxiv.org/abs/2003.07402, Section 2. The hard part is that general multiset diagonal coinvariants lack a direct basis; many older hook results concern hooks on the S_n-Frobenius side, not hooks in the grading-variable GL side.

directions.name: Primary route: GL(1|1) hook detector plus Fields theorem
directions.description: Use the proven diagonal supersymmetry theorem to reduce ordinary GL-hook coefficients to the one-boson/one-fermion superspace coinvariant ring. For lambda=(a,1^b), a>=1, the resulting positive interpretation should be: c_(a,1^b)(n) counts triples (mu,T,U,pi), where mu is a partition of n, T and U are standard Young tableaux of shape mu, des(T)>=b+1, pi is a partition inside a b by (des(T)-1-b) rectangle, and a=maj(T)-b des(T)+binom(b,2)+|pi|. Equivalently, the refined Frobenius coefficient c_(a,1^b),mu omits the auxiliary tableau U. For b=0 this excludes the unique descent-free row tableau, accounting for the constant term.
key_ideas: Diagonal supersymmetry says the same universal coefficients c_lambda appear in Hilb(R_n^(1,1);q,u) expanded in super Schur functions s_lambda(q/u).
In one even and one odd variable, s_(a,1^b)(q/u)=q^a u^b+q^(a-1)u^(b+1), and non-hooks vanish.
Fields gives the q,z Frobenius of superspace coinvariants in fermionic degree b as C_{n,n-b}(x;q), whose Schur expansion is a standard-tableau major-index/q-binomial formula.
The q-Pascal identity [d choose b]_q=[d-1 choose b]_q+q^(d-b)[d-1 choose b-1]_q exactly inverts the triangular relation H_b=delta_{b,0}+C_b+q^(-1)C_{b-1}.
relevant_techniques: Diagonal Supersymmetry for Coinvariant Rings, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.14885, Theorem 1.2, Corollary 1.3, and Proposition 5.1: universal super-Schur Hilbert and Frobenius coefficients are independent of the numbers of bosonic and fermionic variable sets.
Diagonal Supersymmetry for Coinvariant Rings, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.14885, Lemma 4.4: the GL(1|1) super Schur specialization kills non-hooks and gives the two-term formula for hooks.

A Proof of the Fields Conjectures, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.24027, Equations (2.5)-(2.10) and Theorem 5.2: grFrob(SR_n;q,z)=sum_k C_{n,k}(x;q) z^(n-k), with C_{n,k} having the standard-tableau Schur expansion.

Ordered Set Partitions, Generalized Coinvariant Algebras, and the Delta Conjecture, https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.07575, Corollary 6.13: the same kind of Schur expansion by standard tableaux and q-binomials, useful for checking indexing conventions.
directions.potential_obstacles: The main danger is an indexing/sign convention error: Paper 14 uses super Schur s_lambda(q/u), Paper 15 uses fermionic degree n-k, and older Delta papers sometimes apply omega or q-reversal. Also, the row case b=0 has a constant term that must be removed; otherwise the formula incorrectly counts the trivial GL summand.

directions.name: Direct superspace Artin-basis model
directions.description: Translate the hook coefficients into an explicit count of superspace Artin basis monomials, then construct a sign-reversing cancellation for the triangular subtraction. The superspace basis gives H_b(q)=sum_{|J|=b} product_i [st(J)_i]_q. The hook polynomial C_b(q)=sum_a c_(a,1^b)(n) q^a satisfies C_b=H_b-delta_{b,0}-q^(-1)C_{b-1}. A direct basis-level proof would identify the unpaired monomials in this recurrence.
key_ideas: Use theta-support J as fermionic degree and staircase bounds 0<=alpha_i<st(J)_i as the monomial model.
Interpret q^(-1)C_{b-1} as a degree-shifted shadow of lower fermionic degree, induced by the second term q^(a-1)u^(b+1) in s_(a,1^b)(q/u).
Seek a cancellation/injection from lower-degree shadows into the |J|=b basis elements, leaving a positive set equivalent to the tableau formula.
relevant_techniques: The Hilbert Series of the Superspace Coinvariant Ring, https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.09763, Theorem 5.3 and Lemma 2.1: Hilb(SR_n;q,z)=sum_J z^|J| product_i [st(J)_i]_q=sum_k z^(n-k)[k]!_q Stir_q(n,k).

Superspace Coinvariants and Hyperplane Arrangements, https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.17919, Theorem 7.1 and Corollary 7.2: the Sagan-Swanson Artin monomials M(J) theta_J form an actual basis, not just a Hilbert-series model.

The Hilbert Series of the Superspace Coinvariant Ring, https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.09763, Theorem 4.12: the theta_J sector is controlled by the colon quotient (I_n:f_J), explaining why the staircase product is algebraically natural.

Diagonal Supersymmetry for Coinvariant Rings, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.14885, Corollary 5.3: the q-Stirling cancellation identity Hilb(R_n^(1,1);q,-q)=1 is the global shadow of the same cancellation mechanism.
directions.potential_obstacles: The recurrence is positive only after cancellation; the Artin basis alone gives H_b, not C_b. A clean degree-preserving involution or injection may be nontrivial. If the goal is just a combinatorial interpretation, the tableau formula in the primary route is faster and already positive.

directions.name: Ordered-set-partition and Delta-specialization interpretation
directions.description: Repackage the tableau formula in terms of ordered multiset partitions or ordered set partitions. Fields identifies the fermionic degree n-k piece of SR_n with C_{n,k}(x;q), and C_{n,k} has equivalent ordered multiset partition formulas using inv, maj, dinv, and minimaj. One can try to express c_(a,1^b)(n) as a statistic on ordered set partitions with an additional choice corresponding to the q-binomial [des(T)-1 choose b]_q.
key_ideas: Use standardization and Gessel fundamental expansions to pass between ordered multiset partitions and tableau descent data.
Apply the same q-Pascal truncation that turns [des choose b]_q into [des-1 choose b]_q.
relevant_techniques: A Proof of the Fields Conjectures, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.24027, Equations (2.5)-(2.10): C_{n,k} equals Delta'_{e_{k-1}} e_n at t=0 and has ordered multiset partition interpretations with inv, maj, dinv, and minimaj.

A Proof of the Fields Conjectures, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.24027, Theorem 5.2 and Corollary 5.3: fermionic degree n-k of SR_n has Frobenius C_{n,k} and, ungraded, is the sign-twisted ordered-set-partition module.

Ordered Set Partitions, Generalized Coinvariant Algebras, and the Delta Conjecture, https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.07575, Equations (2.19)-(2.23) and Corollary 6.12: ordered multiset partition expansions and their Frobenius/Hilbert transformations.

Ordered Set Partitions, Generalized Coinvariant Algebras, and the Delta Conjecture, https://arxiv.org/abs/1609.07575, Gessel fundamental expansion technique: standardize reading words to connect raw statistics to Schur/tableau formulas.
directions.potential_obstacles: The ordered-set-partition model naturally describes H_b, not the hook coefficient C_b. The q-binomial truncation has an immediate tableau meaning but may look artificial in ordered partition language unless one builds an explicit marked-descent or block-choice statistic.

directions.name: Refined Frobenius-level proof before Hilbert specialization
directions.description: Prove the stronger statement for universal Frobenius coefficients c_(a,1^b),mu first, then obtain c_(a,1^b)(n) by summing over mu with weight f^mu. This keeps the S_n structure visible and avoids losing information in the Hilbert specialization.
key_ideas: For each mu, define C_{b,mu}(q)=sum_{T in SYT(mu), des(T)>=b+1} q^(maj(T)-b des(T)+binom(b,2)) [des(T)-1 choose b]_q.
Use q-Pascal to verify K_{b,mu}=delta_{b,0}delta_{mu,(n)}+C_{b,mu}+q^(-1)C_{b-1,mu}, where K_{b,mu} is the coefficient of s_mu in the fermionic-degree-b superspace Frobenius series.
relevant_techniques: Diagonal Supersymmetry for Coinvariant Rings, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.14885, Corollary 1.3: universal Frobenius coefficients c_{lambda,mu} exist in the super-Schur expansion.

A Proof of the Fields Conjectures, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.24027, Lemma 2.2: parabolic antisymmetrizers compute e_mu^perp of Frobenius characteristics, useful if one wants an independent representation-theoretic verification.

A Proof of the Fields Conjectures, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.24027, Lemma 4.19: antisymmetrized Artin-basis representatives give exact dimensions for parabolic probes, offering a proof route that does not rely only on symmetric-function manipulation.

Multivariate diagonal coinvariant spaces for complex reflection groups, https://arxiv.org/abs/1105.4358, Theorem 2.2 and small-n data: useful sanity checks for the recovered universal Frobenius coefficients.
directions.potential_obstacles: This is slightly stronger than the asked Hilbert-series statement and therefore has more notation. However, it is often cleaner: the q-binomial identity is componentwise in mu, and the final Hilbert coefficient is just the standard dimension-weighted sum.

directions.name: Use two-variable parking and Schrodinger-path results only as checks
directions.description: Do not start from the classical two-boson shuffle/Schroeder path hook formulas; they usually concern hooks in the S_n Frobenius alphabet, while the problem asks for hooks in the GL grading alphabet. They are still valuable for testing the specialization of the final c_(a,1^b)(n) formula to two grading variables.
key_ideas: Specialize the universal Schur expansion to two variables q,t and compare small n with known Hilb(DR_n;q,t).
Use parking-function and Schroeder-path scalar-product formulas to check e/h or S_n-hook projections, but keep them separate from GL-hook extraction.
relevant_techniques: Schedules and the Delta Conjecture, https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.04732, Theorem 4.13: Schroeder paths give hook Schur coefficients of nabla e_n on the Frobenius side, a useful but different hook problem.

A Combinatorial Formula for the Character of the Diagonal Coinvariants, https://arxiv.org/abs/math/0310424, Sections 4.5-4.7: parking-function and Schroeder specializations of scalar products with e_eta h_mu.

A polynomial expression for the Hilbert series of the quotient ring of diagonal coinvariants, https://www2.math.upenn.edu/~jhaglund/preprints/poly3.pdf, Corollary 1 and Section 5.3: Tesler/constant-term formulas provide independent q,t Hilbert checks.
directions.potential_obstacles: This route can easily conflate two different Schur bases. It is best used after the supersymmetry derivation, as validation rather than as the main proof.

key_obstacles: The central obstacle is not existence of the hook coefficients but making the GL-hook extraction positive and conventionally correct. Older diagonal-coinvariant literature gives many hook formulas, but most are for S_n-Frobenius hooks or for two grading variables. The decisive modern input is the proven diagonal supersymmetry theorem, which turns GL hooks into GL(1|1) superspace degrees. After that, one must carefully remove the two-term overlap s_(a,1^b)(q/u)=q^a u^b+q^(a-1)u^(b+1). The q-Pascal identity gives a clean positive tableau formula; a more direct Artin-basis or ordered-set-partition interpretation would require an additional explicit cancellation or marking construction.
recommended_starting_point: Start with the primary route. Write H_b,mu(q)=sum_{T in SYT(mu)} q^(maj(T)-b des(T)+binom(b,2)) [des(T) choose b]_q from A Proof of the Fields Conjectures, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.24027, Theorem 5.2. Define C_b,mu(q)=sum_{T: des(T)>=b+1} q^(maj(T)-b des(T)+binom(b,2)) [des(T)-1 choose b]_q. Check the identity H_b,mu=delta_{b,0}delta_{mu,(n)}+C_b,mu+q^(-1)C_{b-1,mu} by q-Pascal. Then invoke Diagonal Supersymmetry for Coinvariant Rings, https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.14885, Lemma 4.4 and Proposition 5.1, to identify [q^a]C_b,mu with the universal Frobenius hook coefficient, and sum over mu with weight f^mu to obtain the requested Hilbert coefficient c_(a,1^b)(n).
